title: ICU antibiotic plan documentation-only
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports entered suspected infection, source and risk-factor context.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1–1.2 — recognition, suspected infection, source, and risk factors

investigations.text: Supports clinician-selected blood and microbiology testing, urinalysis, chest radiography, and abdominal or pelvic imaging to identify an infection source.
investigations.rationale: Supports clinician-selected source investigation and result review without choosing antimicrobial therapy.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-selected blood and microbiology testing, urinalysis, chest radiography, and abdominal or pelvic imaging to identify an infection source.

source_limitations: Source, cultures, susceptibilities, allergies, organ function, antimicrobial, dose, route and review date are absent.
No antibiotic, dose, duration, de-escalation or stop decision is generated.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
